title: Jamie Hunter wins the US Women's Open as first openly trans snooker champion
description: English snooker player Jamie Hunter took the US Women's Open title, the first women's tour ranking event won by an openly transgender player.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Zander Murray comes out as Scotland's first openly gay senior male footballer
description: Zander Murray announced he is gay, the first senior male soccer player in Scotland to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Anthony Bowens becomes the first openly gay All Elite Wrestling champion
description: Professional wrestler Anthony Bowens won a championship in All Elite Wrestling, the first openly gay wrestler to hold a title in the promotion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lucas Krzikalla is first openly gay active male pro team athlete in Germany
description: Handball player Lucas Krzikalla came out as gay, the first openly gay player in the Handball-Bundesliga and the first active male athlete in any German professional team sport to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Byron Perkins is the first openly gay football player at an HBCU
description: Hampton University player Byron Perkins came out as gay, the first openly gay football player at any Historically Black College or University in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Isaac Humphries becomes Australia's first openly gay male basketball player
description: Isaac Humphries came out as gay, making him the first openly gay Australian male basketball player and the first openly gay player in the National Basketball League.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Justine Lindsay joins an NFL cheerleading squad as its first openly trans member
description: Justine Lindsay joined the Carolina TopCats, becoming the first openly transgender person on a National Football League cheerleading team.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New York City Marathon awards its first non-binary prize money
description: The New York City Marathon paid prize money to non-binary athletes for the first time, with New York Road Runners offering 5,000 dollars to the fastest non-binary finisher alongside the established male and female categories.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jai Vidal signs with Impact Wrestling as its first openly gay male wrestler
description: Jai Vidal joined Impact Wrestling, the first openly gay male wrestler signed by the promotion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kristin Crowley named first openly gay chief of the Los Angeles Fire Department
description: Kristin Crowley took charge of the Los Angeles Fire Department as its first openly gay chief and also its first female chief.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Petros Levounis elected first openly gay American Psychiatric Association head
description: The American Psychiatric Association elected Petros Levounis as its president, the first openly gay person to hold the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Therese Stewart confirmed as first openly lesbian California presiding justice
description: Therese Stewart was confirmed as presiding justice of Division Two of California's 1st District Court of Appeal, the first openly lesbian presiding justice on any appellate court in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hebrew Union College ordains its first nonbinary candidate
description: The Hebrew Union College – Jewish Institute of Religion granted a certificate of ordination to a nonbinary candidate for the first time.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Andorra
description: Andorra's law opening civil marriage to same-sex couples came into force, allowing couples to marry in the principality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Taiwan opens joint adoption to same-sex couples
description: Taiwan extended adoption rights so that married same-sex couples can jointly adopt children, closing a gap left by its 2019 marriage law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cook Islands decriminalizes homosexuality
description: The Cook Islands repealed its colonial-era law criminalizing sexual acts between men, removing the offense from its statute books.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mauritius decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Mauritius ended the criminalization of consensual same-sex acts, with its colonial-era sodomy provision struck from enforcement.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Uganda enacts the Anti-Homosexuality Act 2023
description: Uganda's Anti-Homosexuality Act, 2023 imposed up to twenty years in prison for so-called promotion of homosexuality, life imprisonment for same-sex acts, and the death penalty for what the law terms aggravated homosexuality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Edgars Rinkevics elected as Latvia's and the EU's first openly gay president
description: The Saeima elected Edgars Rinkevics president of Latvia on 31 May 2023, making him the first openly gay head of state in Latvia and in any European Union member state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Sylvia Swayne is the first openly trans woman to run for office in Alabama
description: Sylvia Swayne declared her candidacy for the Alabama House of Representatives, the first openly transgender woman to seek public office in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Toni Atkins is first openly LGBT person to sign a California bill into law
description: Toni Atkins signed a bill into law in California, the first openly LGBT person to do so in the state.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laphonza Butler sworn in as first Black out lesbian in the US Congress
description: Laphonza Butler was sworn into the US Senate on 3 October 2023, becoming the first Black out lesbian in Congress and the first openly LGBT senator from California.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Stefanos Kasselakis becomes first openly gay leader of a major Greek party
description: Stefanos Kasselakis was elected leader of Syriza, the first openly gay person to head a major political party in Greece.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rue Landau elected as Philadelphia's first openly gay city council member
description: Voters elected Rue Landau to the Philadelphia City Council, the first openly gay person to win a seat on the body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kim Petras is first openly trans artist to win a major-category Grammy
description: Kim Petras, together with Sam Smith, won the 2023 Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance for the song Unholy, making her the first openly transgender artist to take a major-category Grammy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harry Potter franchise gets its first transgender character
description: The video game Hogwarts Legacy introduced Sirona Ryan, the first transgender character in the Harry Potter franchise.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rikkie Kolle wins Miss Nederland as first openly trans national pageant winner
description: Rikkie Kolle won the Miss Nederland 2023 pageant, the first openly transgender person to win a national beauty pageant.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ezra Miller is first openly nonbinary lead in a major superhero film
description: With the release of The Flash, Ezra Miller became the first openly nonbinary person to play the lead role in a major superhero franchise film.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Pixar introduces its first non-binary character in Elemental
description: The 2023 Pixar film Elemental featured Lake, the studio's first non-binary character.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Yosha Iglesias qualifies as first openly trans Woman International Master
description: French chess player Yosha Iglesias completed the requirements for the Woman International Master title in December 2023, the first openly transgender player to qualify for it.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hallmark premieres its first lesbian holiday romance film
description: Hallmark aired Friends and Family Christmas, the network's first holiday romance centered on a lesbian couple.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lio Mehiel wins Sundance acting jury award as first openly trans recipient
description: Lio Mehiel won the Sundance Dramatic Special Jury Award for Best Acting for the film Mutt, the first openly trans actor to receive the prize.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Marina Machete is Portugal's first openly trans Miss Universe contestant
description: Marina Machete represented Portugal at the Miss Universe 2023 pageant in El Salvador, the country's first openly transgender contestant, and reached the top 20 as the first openly trans woman to place at the contest.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Campbell Johnstone comes out as the first openly gay All Black
description: Former rugby player Campbell Johnstone said in an interview on the New Zealand show Seven Sharp that he is gay, making him the first openly gay All Black.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Boston Marathon holds its first nonbinary division
description: The Boston Marathon included a nonbinary division for the first time in 2023; Kae Ravichandran won it in a time of 2:38:57.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kevin Maxen is first openly gay male coach in major US men's pro sports
description: Kevin Maxen, an assistant strength and conditioning coach with the Jacksonville Jaguars, came out as gay, the first openly gay male coach in a major American men's professional sports league.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Quinn is the first openly trans and non-binary footballer at a FIFA World Cup
description: Canadian midfielder Quinn played at the FIFA World Cup as the first openly transgender and non-binary footballer to appear at the tournament.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Che Flores is first out nonbinary trans referee in major US pro sports
description: NBA official Che Flores became the first out nonbinary and transgender referee in a major professional sports league in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luke Prokop becomes the first openly gay player in AHL history
description: Luke Prokop made his American Hockey League debut for the Milwaukee Admirals on 17 November 2023, the first openly gay player in the league's history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jesse Ehrenfeld sworn in as first openly gay American Medical Association head
description: Jesse Ehrenfeld took office as president of the American Medical Association on 13 June 2023, the first openly gay person to lead the organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Seth Marnin becomes first openly transgender male judge in the United States
description: Seth Marnin took the bench as the first openly transgender judge in New York and the first openly transgender male judge anywhere in the United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Danica Roem elected first openly trans state senator in the US South
description: Danica Roem won election to the Virginia Senate, the first openly transgender person elected to a state senate in the Southern United States.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Seoul High Court orders spousal health coverage for a same-sex couple
description: The Seoul High Court ruled that the National Health Insurance Service must extend spousal coverage to a same-sex couple, the first South Korean court decision to recognize rights of a same-sex couple.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Inter-American Court rules for complainant in Olivera Fuentes vs. Peru
description: The Inter-American Court of Human Rights issued its ruling in Olivera Fuentes vs. Peru, the first case of sexual-orientation discrimination from the Peruvian justice system to reach the court and the first decision in the inter-American system in favor of a complainant discriminated against by a consumer company for their sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jeanne Marrazzo named first openly gay director of NIAID
description: Jeanne Marrazzo was appointed director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases at the National Institutes of Health, the first openly gay person to head the institute.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jeff R. Johnson elected first openly gay bishop of the Sierra Pacific Synod
description: On 17 September 2023, Jeff R. Johnson was elected bishop of the Sierra Pacific Synod, the first openly gay person chosen for the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Black Cat Tavern becomes first California landmark marker for LGBTQ history
description: On 1 October 2023 the Black Cat Tavern in Los Angeles received its marker as California Historical Landmark number 1063, the first landmark marker in the state tied to LGBTQ history.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Liechtenstein passes a same-sex marriage law
description: Liechtenstein's parliament approved legislation opening civil marriage to same-sex couples in 2024.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Thailand passes a marriage equality law
description: Thailand's legislature approved a law opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2024, making it the first country in Southeast Asia to pass marriage equality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Greece legalizes same-sex marriage
description: Greece's parliament passed a same-sex marriage law in 2024 and the law came into force the same year, making Greece the first Orthodox Christian majority country with marriage equality.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
